text: The Latin version gives ‘the redeemed city, the dove’, but this seems due to a faulty understanding of the Hebrew text. Cf. Jer. 25.38.

Literally, ‘Therefore thou must wait for me’, but the verb commonly expresses an attitude of pious longing, and it looks as if the faithful remnant, not the generality of the Jewish people, were being addressed.
Literally, ‘the day when I will rise up thenceforward’; the Hebrew text has ‘the day when I will rise up and catch my prey’, the Septuagint Greek, ‘the day when I will rise up to bear witness’.
In the Hebrew text, ‘daughters’; there is some ground for suspecting a faulty reading in the manuscripts.
Here again, in verses 17 and 18, most modern editors question the soundness of the Hebrew text; in the Latin version, the difficulties are partly disguised.
